concerns.text: Constitution VI requires flagging any discrepancy greater than an absolute 0.05, yet FR‑004 implements a relative tolerance `max(0.01, 0.2 × reconstructed_p)`. This allows discrepancies >0.05 to be considered consistent when the reconstructed p‑value is large (e.g., p=0.30 → tolerance 0.06). The validation rule therefore conflicts with the non‑negotiable principle and may miss genuine inconsistencies, undermining the scientific claim of exhaustive detection.
